<!-- Chart CH-COR-04 COLUMN + LINE rendered in language DL-12 NOROSHI -->

## COLUMN + LINE chart, NOROSHI style

Render as SVG (or equivalent) on a 960x540 px canvas.
Every position, size, and color below is exact. No adjectives, no improvisation.

### AI metadata (for agent routing)
```json
{
  "code": "CH-COR-04",
  "ovp_version": "1.0",
  "intent": [
    "correlation",
    "change-over-time"
  ],
  "form": "columns on a left scale, one line on a labeled right scale",
  "data_required": "ordered periods + one volume measure + one rate measure",
  "family": "COR",
  "alternatives": [
    "CH-TIM-01 (same units: two lines)",
    "CH-COR-01 (relationship itself)"
  ],
  "see_instead": [
    {
      "when": "both measures share a unit",
      "use": "CH-TIM-01"
    },
    {
      "when": "the relationship, not time, is the question",
      "use": "CH-COR-01"
    }
  ]
}
```

Use when:
- a volume and a rate that move together over time
- capacity vs utilization, output vs quality

Do NOT use when:
- two arbitrary series dual-axed to force a story
- scales chosen to make the lines cross dramatically

### Input data shape
```json
{
  "title": "action title with a number",
  "source": "bottom-left always",
  "x": "ordered period labels",
  "bars": "{name, unit, values} on the left scale",
  "line": "{name, unit, values} on the right scale"
}
```

### Rules
- both scales start at zero, both are labeled with unit AND series name
- bars muted, line primary: the rate is usually the story
- never more than one line: a third scale is a lie

### Never do this
- unlabeled dual axes
- non-zero baselines on either scale
- three or more scales
- two lines on one right axis

### QA before delivering
- [ ] both scales zero-based and labeled
- [ ] series named at the scales
- [ ] one line maximum
- [ ] source present

- Title font: 'Segoe UI',Arial,sans-serif, weight 700
